questions.q: In which Indian city is the Agra Fort located?
questions.explanation: The Agra Fort stands in Agra, in the state of Uttar Pradesh, on the right bank of the Yamuna River.

questions.q: Agra Fort is most famously associated with which Indian empire?
questions.explanation: Agra Fort served as a principal residence of the Mughal emperors from Akbar onward.

questions.q: What is the dominant building material that gives Agra Fort its distinctive colour?
questions.explanation: The massive walls and many structures of Agra Fort are built of red sandstone, earning it the nickname 'Red Fort of Agra'.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor rebuilt Agra Fort in its present form in red sandstone starting in 1565?
questions.explanation: Emperor Akbar began rebuilding the fort in red sandstone around 1565, giving it the form it largely retains today.

questions.q: Agra Fort holds which international designation?
questions.explanation: Agra Fort was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983.

questions.q: Along which river does Agra Fort sit?
questions.explanation: Agra Fort overlooks the Yamuna River, the same river beside which the Taj Mahal stands.

questions.q: Famous monument visible from Agra Fort, built across the Yamuna, is the:
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal can be seen from Agra Fort, and Shah Jahan reportedly gazed at it from there in his final years.

questions.q: Agra Fort is sometimes called by which alternative name?
questions.explanation: Because of its red sandstone construction it is commonly known as the 'Red Fort of Agra'.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor spent his last years confined within Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan was imprisoned in Agra Fort by his son Aurangzeb and spent his final years there.

questions.q: Agra Fort primarily functioned as which type of structure?
questions.explanation: Agra Fort was a walled imperial citadel containing palaces, halls and gardens for the Mughal court.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Agra Fort located?
questions.explanation: Agra, and therefore Agra Fort, lies in the state of Uttar Pradesh in northern India.

questions.q: Which son of Shah Jahan placed him under house arrest in Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb, after winning the war of succession, imprisoned his father Shah Jahan in Agra Fort in 1658.

questions.q: Which white-marble palace within Agra Fort was built by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: The Khas Mahal is among the elegant white-marble palaces Shah Jahan added to Agra Fort.

questions.q: What is the 'Diwan-i-Aam' within Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Aam was the Hall of Public Audience where the emperor heard petitions from common people.

questions.q: What is the 'Diwan-i-Khas' in Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: The Diwan-i-Khas was the Hall of Private Audience used to receive nobles, dignitaries and special guests.

questions.q: The Moti Masjid, or 'Pearl Mosque', inside Agra Fort was built largely of what material?
questions.explanation: The Moti Masjid within Agra Fort, built under Shah Jahan, is constructed of white marble, giving it a pearly appearance.

questions.q: Roughly how long are the perimeter walls of Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort is enclosed by massive walls about 2.5 kilometres (around 1.5 miles) in circumference.

questions.q: Through which gate do visitors today usually enter Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: Although the imposing Delhi Gate was the original ceremonial entrance, tourists today enter Agra Fort through the southern Amar Singh Gate.

questions.q: In which century did Akbar begin the major red-sandstone construction of Agra Fort?
questions.explanation: Akbar's rebuilding of Agra Fort began in 1565, placing the major construction in the 16th century.

questions.q: Which architectural style is Agra Fort a prime example of?
questions.explanation: Agra Fort is a celebrated example of Mughal architecture, blending Islamic, Persian and Indian elements.

questions.q: The fort changed character over generations: Akbar favoured red sandstone, while which successor introduced extensive white marble?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan, known for his love of marble, replaced or added many marble structures within the fort.

questions.q: Akbar's grandfather and founder of the Mughal dynasty, who first took Agra, was:
questions.explanation: Babur, founder of the Mughal Empire, captured Agra after the Battle of Panipat in 1526, before Akbar rebuilt the fort.

questions.q: In which year was Agra Fort inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: Agra Fort was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1983, the same year as the nearby Taj Mahal.

questions.q: The octagonal tower in Agra Fort where Shah Jahan is said to have viewed the Taj Mahal is called the:
questions.explanation: The Musamman Burj, a marble octagonal tower built by Shah Jahan, overlooks the Yamuna toward the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: The 'Sheesh Mahal' within Agra Fort is best known for what feature?
questions.explanation: The Sheesh Mahal, or Palace of Mirrors, has walls decorated with countless small mirror pieces, likely serving as a royal bath or dressing area.

questions.q: Before Akbar's red-sandstone fort, the site held an earlier brick fortress associated with which dynasty?
questions.explanation: An earlier brick fort known as Badalgarh, linked with the Lodi dynasty, stood on the site before Akbar's reconstruction.

questions.q: The 'Anguri Bagh' (Grape Garden) within Agra Fort is laid out in which classic Persian garden style?
questions.explanation: The Anguri Bagh is arranged as a charbagh, the four-quartered Persian garden plan favoured by the Mughals.

questions.q: During which 1857 conflict did Agra Fort serve as a refuge and stronghold for British forces?
questions.explanation: During the Indian Rebellion (or Revolt) of 1857, Agra Fort became a shelter and defensive position for British residents and troops.

questions.q: The Nagina Masjid, a small private mosque in Agra Fort, was built for whom under Shah Jahan?
options: The ladies of the royal harem
questions.explanation: The marble Nagina Masjid was built as a private mosque for the ladies of the imperial harem.

questions.q: The Mughal capital was located at Agra during the reigns of several emperors before being shifted to which city by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan moved the Mughal capital from Agra to his new city of Shahjahanabad (Old Delhi) around 1648.
